title: dillwynia floribunda var. teretifolia
intro: Dillwynia floribunda var. teretifolia, also known as the Australian prickle bush, is a shrub native to Australia, characterized by its compact growth and small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Dillwynia floribunda var. teretifolia include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the skin, as well as support overall digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used topically to treat minor skin ailments and internally to promote digestive well-being.
